The '''78th British Academy Film Awards''', more commonly known as the [[British Academy Film Awards|BAFTAs]], were held on 16 February 2025, honouring the best national and foreign films of [[2024 in film|2024]], at the [[Royal Festival Hall]] within [[London]]'s [[Southbank Centre]]. Presented by the [[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]], accolades were given for the best feature-length films and documentaries of any nationality that were screened at British cinemas in 2024.<ref>{{cite web |date=16 February 2025 |title=BAFTAs 2025: The Winners List in Full |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cp3j3wdxxxgo |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250218121421/https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cp3j3wdxxxgo |archive-date=18 February 2025 |url-status=live |access-date=16 February 2025 |publisher=[[BBC]] |language=en-GB}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |date=16 February 2025 |title=BAFTAs 2025: The Full List of Winners |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2025/feb/16/baftas-2025-the-full-list-of-winners-live |access-date=16 February 2025 |newspaper=[[The Guardian]] |ISSN=0261-3077 |language=en-GB}}</ref><ref name="Winners">{{cite web |last=Saunders |first=Emma |date=16 February 2025 |title='Conclave' and 'The Brutalist' Win Big at the BAFTAs |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cvge2gdle6ko |access-date=16 February 2025 |publisher=[[BBC]] |language=en-GB}}</ref>

The BAFTA longlists were unveiled on 3 January 2025.<ref>{{cite press release |url=https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/2025-ee-bafta-film-awards-longlists |title=BAFTA Announces Longlists for the 2025 EE BAFTA Film Awards |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts|BAFTA]] |date=3 January 2025 |access-date=3 January 2025}}</ref> The [[BAFTA Rising Star Award|EE Rising Star Award]] nominees, which is the only category voted for by the British public, were unveiled on 7 January 2025. The nominations for all the other categories were unveiled on 15 January 2025 by previous BAFTA Award winners [[Mia McKenna-Bruce]] and [[Will Sharpe]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2025/01/marisa-abela-mikey-madison-jharrel-jerome-bafta-rising-star-award-nominees-2025-1236249689/ |title=Marisa Abela, Mikey Madison & Jharrel Jerome Among BAFTA Rising Star Award Nominees |last=Ntim |first=Zac |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=7 January 2025 |access-date=8 January 2025 |language=en-US}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2025/awards/awards/how-to-watch-bafta-film-awards-nominations-2025-1236274074/ |title=How to Watch the 2025 BAFTA Film Awards Nominations Online |last=Ritman |first=Alex |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=15 January 2025 |access-date=15 January 2025 |language=en-US}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2025/02/13/movies/baftas-rising-star-award.html |title=At the BAFTAs, Fans Have a Say Who Wins This Award |last=Goldmann |first=A.J. |newspaper=[[The New York Times]] |date=13 February 2025 |access-date=13 February 2025 |language=en-US}}</ref>

The Spanish-language French [[Musical film|musical]] [[Crime film|crime]] film ''[[Emilia Pérez]]'' received fifteen nods in the longlists, followed by ''[[Conclave (film)|Conclave]]'' with fourteen.<ref name="Longlists">{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2025/01/bafta-film-awards-longlist-emilia-perez-conclave-1236245656/ |title=BAFTA Longlists Revealed: 'Emilia Perez' & 'Conclave' Lead the Pack |last=Ntim |first=Zac |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=3 January 2025 |access-date=3 January 2025}}</ref> ''Conclave'' went on to receive the most nominations with twelve, followed by ''Emilia Pérez'' with eleven and ''[[The Brutalist]]'' with nine.<ref name= "Nominees">{{cite web |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cvg969dx33lo |title=Pope drama and Spanish-language musical lead BAFTA race |last=Rufo |first=Yasmin |publisher=[[BBC]] |date=15 January 2025 |access-date=16 January 2025}}</ref> ''The Brutalist'' and ''Conclave'' ultimately won the most awards with four each.<ref name="Winners"/>

The BAFTA longlists were announced on 3 January 2025.<ref name="Longlists"/> The nominees were announced on 7 January 2025.<ref name="Nominees"/> The winners were announced on 16 February 2025.<ref name="Winners"/>

===BAFTA Fellowship===
{{Main|BAFTA Fellowship}}

The BAFTA Fellowship recipient was announced on 10 January 2025.<ref>{{cite press release |title=Warwick Davis to be honoured with BAFTA Fellowship |date=10 January 2025 |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts|BAFTA]] |url=https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/warwick-davis-to-be-honoured-with-bafta-fellowship |access-date=11 January 2025}}</ref>

* '''[[Warwick Davis]]'''

===Outstanding British Contribution to Cinema===
{{Main|BAFTA Outstanding British Contribution to Cinema Award}}

The Outstanding Contribution Award recipient was announced on 6 February 2025.<ref>{{cite press release |title=MediCinema to be honoured with BAFTA's Outstanding British Contribution to Cinema Award |date=6 February 2025 |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts|BAFTA]] |url=https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/medicinema-obcc-2025 |access-date=6 February 2025}}</ref>

* '''[[MediCinema]]'''

==Ceremony information==
[[File:Royal Festival Hall (37366103611).jpg|thumb|upright=2.0|right|The interior of the [[Royal Festival Hall]], which hosted the ceremony]]

The ceremony took place at the [[Royal Festival Hall]] within [[London]]'s [[Southbank Centre]] on 16 February 2025, hosted by [[David Tennant]] for the second year in a row.<ref>{{cite web |last=Roxborough |first=Scott |date=19 April 2024 |title=BAFTA Confirms Date for 2025 Awards |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/bafta-2025-dates-1235877491/ |access-date=3 January 2025 |work=[[The Hollywood Reporter]] |language=en-US}}</ref><ref name="Key Dates">{{cite press release |title=BAFTA unveils the categories, voting, rules and eligibility for the 2025 EE BAFTA Film Awards |date=1 August 2024 |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts|BAFTA]] |url=https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/bafta-unveils-the-categories-voting-rules-and-eligibility-for-the-2025-ee-bafta-film-awards |access-date=3 January 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |last=Ritman |first=Alex |date=8 December 2024 |title=David Tennant to Return as BAFTA Film Awards Host |url=https://variety.com/2024/film/global/bafta-film-awards-2025-host-david-tennant-1236242573/ |access-date=3 January 2025 |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |language=en-US}}</ref> [[British Academy of Film and Television Arts|BAFTA]] digital channels broadcast pre- and post-ceremony content.<ref name="GuestsPresentersAttendees">{{cite press release |title=Special Guests, Presenters and Attendees Unveiled for the EE BAFTA Film Awards |date=13 February 2025 |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts|BAFTA]] |url=https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/special-guests-presenters-and-attendees-unveiled-for-the-ee-bafta-film-awards |access-date=13 February 2025}}</ref> The ceremony was broadcast as [[Broadcast delay|deferred live]], with an approximately one-hour delay, on [[BBC One]] in the United Kingdom and on various [[BritBox]] platforms internationally.<ref name="Performances">{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2025/film/news/take-that-jeff-goldblum-bafta-film-awards-1236297629/ |title=Take That Set to Rock BAFTA Film Awards with 'Greatest Day' Performance; Jeff Goldblum to Play In Memoriam Tribute |last=Ramachandran |first=Naman |author-link=Naman Ramachandran |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=5 February 2025 |access-date=6 February 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite press release |title=David Tennant to return as Host of the 2025 EE BAFTA Film Awards |date=9 December 2024 |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts|BAFTA]] |url=https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/david-tennant-to-return-as-host-of-the-2025-ee-bafta-film-awards |access-date=3 January 2025}}</ref> In his introduction to the ceremony, Tennant performed "[[I'm Gonna Be (500 Miles)]]" by [[The Proclaimers]] before his monologue full of [[dad joke]]s; his scripted gags were of a similar tone throughout. Though the ceremony overran significantly, editing kept the broadcast ceremony to a "brisk" two hours.<ref name="BAFTAs 2025">{{cite news |last1=Mumford |first1=Gwilym |last2=Cochrane |first2=Lauren |last3=Shoard |first3=Catherine |last4=Pulver |first4=Andrew |last5=Khomami |first5=Nadia |date=16 February 2025 |title=BAFTAs 2025: The Red Carpet, the Ceremony, the Winners – as it happened |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/live/2025/feb/16/baftas-2025-the-red-carpet-the-ceremony-the-winners-live |access-date=16 February 2025 |newspaper=[[The Guardian]] |ISSN=0261-3077}}</ref><ref name="Snubs and Surprises">{{cite news |last1=Shoard |first1=Catherine |last2=Mumford |first2=Gwilym |last3=Pulver |first3=Andrew |date=16 February 2025 |title=Demi Moore, Ralph Fiennes and Gromit: Nine Key BAFTA Snubs and Surprises |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2025/feb/16/demi-moore-ralph-fiennes-and-gromit-nine-key-bafta-snubs-and-surprises |access-date=16 February 2025 |newspaper=[[The Guardian]] |ISSN=0261-3077}}</ref>

The [[BAFTA Award for Best Children's & Family Film|Best Children's & Family Film]] was awarded for the first time in 2025, having been announced in 2023 as the first new category since the introduction of [[BAFTA Award for Best Casting|Best Casting]] in [[73rd British Academy Film Awards|2020]].<ref name="New Category"/> During the ceremony, ''[[Deadline Hollywood]]'' suggested there was "a little too much overlap" between it and the [[BAFTA Award for Best Animated Film|Best Animated Film]] category.<ref name="Deadline">{{cite web |last1=Tartaglione |first1=Nancy |last2=Wise |first2=Damon |last3=Kanter |first3=Jake |date=16 February 2025 |title=Deadline's BAFTA Awards Live Blog |url=https://deadline.com/2025/02/bafta-awards-2025-live-updates-1236290471/ |access-date=16 February 2025 |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |language=en-US}}</ref> Other changes to the awards for 2025 include the introduction of new requirements for nomination to the [[BAFTA Award for Best Film|Best Film]] and [[BAFTA Award for Outstanding British Film|Outstanding British Film]] categories.<ref name="Key Dates"/><ref name="New Category">{{cite web |last=Shafer |first=Ellise |date=1 August 2024 |title=BAFTA Film Awards Adds New Children's and Family Category, Introduces Points System for Outstanding British Film Eligibility |url=https://variety.com/2024/film/awards/bafta-film-awards-changes-new-childrens-family-category-british-film-points-system-1236093736/ |access-date=3 January 2025 |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |language=en-US}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |last=Ntim |first=Zac |date=1 August 2024 |title=BAFTA Adds Kids Film Award & Expands Theatrical Requirements for Best Film in Latest Eligibility Updates |url=https://deadline.com/2024/08/bafta-film-awards-2024-eligibility-best-film-childrens-and-family-film-award-1236029870/ |access-date=3 January 2025 |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |language=en-US}}</ref>

{{Wikinews|78th British Academy Film Awards held in London}}

The nominees in 2025 made it the most [[genre]]-diverse ceremony, with horror and musicals well-represented among more traditional award ceremony genres.<ref name="Hot Topics">{{cite web |url=https://www.screendaily.com/features/11-hot-topics-from-the-bafta-nominations-voters-embrace-genre-films-cannes-premieres-gen-z-stars/5200817.article |title=11 Hot Topics from the BAFTA Nominations: Voters embrace genre films, Cannes premieres, Gen Z stars |last1=Dalton |first1=Ben |last2=Gant |first2=Charles |last3=Mueller |first3=Matt |last4=Tabbara |first4=Mona |last5=Calnan |first5=Ellie |last6=Dams |first6=Tim |work=[[Screen International#Screen Daily|Screen Daily]] |date=15 January 2025 |access-date=13 February 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/selena-gomez-zoe-saldana-jesse-eisenberg-ralph-fiennes-bafta-awards-presenters-1236135411/ |title=Zoe Saldaña, Selena Gomez, Jesse Eisenberg, Ralph Fiennes, Joe Alwyn Among BAFTA Film Awards Presenters |last=Ford |first=Lily |work=[[The Hollywood Reporter]] |date=13 February 2025 |access-date=13 February 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.screendaily.com/features/are-awards-voters-finally-embracing-genre-titles/5201525.article |title=Are awards voters finally embracing genre titles? |last=Mitchell |first=Wendy |work=[[Screen International#Screen Daily|Screen Daily]] |date=5 February 2025 |access-date=13 February 2025}}</ref> In terms of individual nominees, ''[[Anora]]'' filmmaker [[Sean Baker]] earned the second-most nominations for an individual at a single ceremony with five (Best Film, [[BAFTA Award for Best Direction|Best Director]], [[BAFTA Award for Best Original Screenplay|Best Original Screenplay]], Best Casting, and [[BAFTA Award for Best Editing|Best Editing]])<ref name="Hot Topics"/> and filmmaker [[Rich Peppiatt]] became the most-nominated debut director at the BAFTAs with four personal nominations for ''[[Kneecap (film)|Kneecap]]''.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/kneecap-bafta-film-awards-most-nominated-debut-film-rich-peppiatt-1236109360/ |title=Rich Peppiatt Makes BAFTA History as Most Nominated Debut Director Ever with 'Kneecap' |last=Ford |first=Lily |work=[[The Hollywood Reporter]] |date=15 January 2025 |access-date=13 February 2025}}</ref>

During the technical awards, the ceremony sound cut out as [[BAFTA Award for Best Sound|Best Sound]] was presented, though this was fixed for broadcast.<ref name="Deadline"/> Multiple winners were noted for their lack of acceptance speech preparation and the endearingly rambling comments they made instead, including [[Nick Park]] (for ''[[Wallace & Gromit: Vengeance Most Fowl]]'') and [[Jesse Eisenberg]] (for ''[[A Real Pain]]''). Ahead of the acting and other major awards being announced,<ref name="BAFTAs 2025"/> [[Take That]] performed their song "[[Greatest Day (Take That song)|Greatest Day]]", which featured prominently in nominee ''Anora''.<ref name="Performances"/> Two awards later, the first of two montages of other awards to not receive as-live broadcasts was shown; this included ''[[Emilia Pérez]]'' winning [[BAFTA Award for Best Film Not in the English Language|Best Film Not in the English Language]], the clip notably cutting out director [[Jacques Audiard]] paying tribute to controversial star [[Karla Sofía Gascón]] in his speech. Winners considered surprising were [[David Jonsson]] for the [[BAFTA Rising Star Award|EE Rising Star Award]] and [[Mikey Madison]] winning [[BAFTA Award for Best Actress in a Leading Role|Best Actress in a Leading Role]], while ''[[A Complete Unknown]]'' failing to win any of its six nominations was considered a surprising snub.<ref name="BAFTAs 2025"/><ref name="Snubs and Surprises"/> Favourites in the supporting performance categories, [[Zoe Saldaña]] (for ''Emilia Pérez'') and [[Kieran Culkin]] (for ''A Real Pain'') won their categories, which was said to make them all but guaranteed to win at the [[97th Academy Awards]].<ref>{{cite news |last=Shoard |first=Catherine |date=16 February 2025 |title=BAFTAs 2025: 'Conclave' beats 'The Brutalist' to Best Picture as Mikey Madison scoops Best Actress |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2025/feb/16/baftas-2025-conclave-beats-the-brutalist-to-best-picture-as-mikey-madison-surprises-as-best-actress-winner |access-date=16 February 2025 |newspaper=[[The Guardian]] |language=en-GB |ISSN=0261-3077}}</ref>

There were relatively few political comments made at the ceremony,<ref name="BAFTAs 2025"/><ref>{{cite web |last1=Ritman |first1=Alex |last2=Shafer |first2=Ellise |date=16 February 2025 |title=BAFTA Awards: 'Conclave' Scores Best Film as 'The Brutalist' Ties With Four Wins and Mikey Madison Surprises in Leading Actress |url=https://variety.com/2025/film/awards/bafta-film-awards-2025-winners-list-1236302717/ |access-date=16 February 2025 |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |language=en-US}}</ref> with Tennant making jokes about [[Donald Trump]]—though some, including comparing Trump to the character Beetlejuice, as well as jokes about ''[[Conclave (film)|Conclave]]'', were cut from the broadcast;<ref name="Snubs and Surprises"/><ref name="Joke Cut">{{cite web |last=Shafer |first=Ellise |date=16 February 2025 |title=David Tennant's Donald Trump-Beetlejuice Joke Cut from BBC BAFTAs Broadcast |url=https://variety.com/2025/film/awards/david-tennant-trump-beetlejuice-joke-cut-bbc-baftas-broadcast-1236310226/ |access-date=16 February 2025 |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |language=en-US}}</ref> Peppiatt saying that everyone "should have their language respected, their culture respected and their homeland respected"; and [[Edward Berger]] accepting Outstanding British Film for ''Conclave'' by vaguely referring to [[2025 German federal election|elections in Germany]] and describing the time as "a crisis of democracy". When presenting Best Film, [[Mark Hamill]] referred to "troubling times" before deliberately not naming Trump.<ref name="BAFTAs 2025"/> In reference to which comments were cut, the [[BBC]] stated editing decisions were "made due to time constraints".<ref name="Joke Cut"/>

==In Memoriam==
The In Memoriam was accompanied by actor [[Jeff Goldblum]], performing "[[As Time Goes By (song)|As Time Goes By]]" on piano, and paid tribute to the following:<ref>{{cite web |last=Kanter |first=Jake |date=16 February 2025 |title=Jeff Goldblum Plays Moving Piano Tribute to David Lynch, Maggie Smith & Others During BAFTA Film Awards In Memoriam Section |url=https://deadline.com/2025/02/bafta-awards-2025-in-memoriam-jeff-goldblum-david-lynch-maggie-smith-1236291705/ |access-date=16 February 2025 |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |language=en-US}}</ref>
